---
title: Course Resources
bibliography: SISMID-Module.bib
nocite: "@*"
---

# Course materials and downloads

The most reliable option is to download the full course repository. That gives
you the data, exercises, source files, slides, and project file in one folder.

1. Go to the [SISMID-2026 GitHub repository](https://github.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026).
1. Click **Code**.
1. Click **Download ZIP**.
1. Unzip the folder.
1. Open `SISMID-2026.Rproj`.

Direct full-repository download:
[SISMID-2026 main branch ZIP](https://github.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/archive/refs/heads/main.zip)

If you use Git:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026.git
```

## Individual data files

Use the full repository ZIP if you can. If you only need one data file, use
these direct links:

| Data file | Download |
|---|---|
| QCRC COVID-19 ICU workbook | [QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx](https://github.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/raw/main/data/QCRC_FINAL_Deidentified.xlsx) |
| Measles vaccination and case data | [measles_final.Rds](https://github.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/raw/main/data/measles_final.Rds) |
| Simulated city cases | [city-cases.csv](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/data/city-cases.csv) |
| SIR observed data | [SIR-observed.csv](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/data/SIR-observed.csv) |
| Mosquito surveillance data | [mos-nyc.csv](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/data/mos-nyc.csv) |

Save downloaded data files inside the repository's `data/` folder unless an
exercise tells you otherwise.

For dataset descriptions and recommended uses, see the
[Dataset Catalog](support/dataset-catalog.qmd).

## Exercises and report files

You can view exercises from the site navigation. To edit them locally, download
the full repository ZIP or use these source-file links:

| File | Download source |
|---|---|
| Exercise 1 | [Exercise1.qmd](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/exercises/Exercise1.qmd) |
| Exercise 2 | [Exercise2.qmd](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/exercises/Exercise2.qmd) |
| QCRC starter template | [qcrc-mini-manuscript-starter.qmd](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/exercises/qcrc-mini-manuscript-starter.qmd) |
| QCRC checklist and rubric | [qcrc-report-checklist-rubric.qmd](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/wzbillings/SISMID-2026/main/exercises/qcrc-report-checklist-rubric.qmd) |

If a raw `.qmd` file opens in your browser, use **Save As** to save it into the
repository's `exercises/` folder.

## Slides and module files

The online slide decks are linked in the site navigation. There is not a
separate PDF bundle in this repository right now.

For offline use:

- download the full repository ZIP,
- open `SISMID-2026.Rproj`,
- use the `.qmd` files in `modules/`,
- render locally if you need a fresh HTML copy.

If you want a PDF copy of a deck during class, use the browser print dialog or
ask an instructor which output format is best for that deck.

# Learner support pages

- [Setup check](support/setup-check.qmd): confirm R, Quarto, packages, and data
  access.
- [Dataset catalog](support/dataset-catalog.qmd): choose and document course
  datasets.
- [Troubleshooting checklist](support/troubleshooting.qmd): work through common
  R, Quarto, data, and model errors.
- [Model-reporting cheat sheet](support/model-reporting.qmd): report `lm()`,
  `glm()`, confidence intervals, odds ratios, and predictions clearly.
- [Loops/functions/apply guide](support/loops-functions-apply.qmd): decide when
  to use vectorized code, a function, a loop, or an apply-family tool.

# Useful (+ Free) Resources

- R for Data Science: https://r4ds.hadley.nz/  
(great general information)
- Fundamentals of Data Visualization: https://clauswilke.com/dataviz/ 
- R for Epidemiology: https://www.r4epi.com/
- The Epidemiologist R Handbook: https://epirhandbook.com/en/
- R basics by Rafael A. Irizarry: https://rafalab.github.io/dsbook/r-basics.html
(great general information)
- Open Case Studies: https://www.opencasestudies.org/  
(resource for specific public health cases with statistical implementation and interpretation)
- Tidytuesday: https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday (data for R practice)

**Slides on making tables**
https://github.com/wzbillings/idig-tables

**Book on SIR-type modeling with differential equations**
https://andreashandel.github.io/IDEMAbook/

# Need help?

- Various "Cheat Sheets": [https://github.com/rstudio/cheatsheets/](https://github.com/rstudio/cheatsheets/)
- R reference card: [http://cran.r-project.org/doc/contrib/Short-refcard.pdf](http://cran.r-project.org/doc/contrib/Short-refcard.pdf)  
- R jargon: [https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/bbm%3A978-1-4419-1318-0%2F1.pdf](https://link.springer.com/content/pdf/bbm%3A978-1-4419-1318-0%2F1.pdf)
- R terminology: [https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-release/R-lang.pdf](https://cran.r-project.org/doc/manuals/r-release/R-lang.pdf)


# Other references {.unnumbered}

::: {#refs}
:::
